## Summary

This October 2025 guide from Averi.ai is the most directly relevant source for the cadence-planning guide because it explicitly addresses the solo founder use case and provides specific time investment estimates. The core framing is important: "Content planning at the startup level means one person or founder building a system that runs in 5 hours per week - not a 20-person content team following a 90-day editorial calendar." This restated framing (5 hours/week as the budgetary constraint, not pages/month) is more realistic and actionable than most content strategy advice.

The guide provides specific cadence recommendations by team size: solo founder at 1-2 articles/week using 5 hours total; founder + AI content engine at 2-3 articles/week in 5-8 hours; small marketing team (2-3 people) at 3-5 articles/week. These are the most specific cadence-by-team-size figures in the research set.

The guide confirms the pillar-first publishing strategy: build the strategy map (clusters, pillar pages, internal linking skeleton) first, then the calendar is an output of the strategy, not the starting point. A critically important stat: "teams planning 2-3 months ahead see 41% higher success rates." The 70/30 content split (70% evergreen cluster content, 30% timely/reactive pieces) is a practical planning heuristic for founders building a content queue. The guide also introduces "dual discovery planning" - every piece planned for both Google search and AI platforms (ChatGPT, Perplexity, AI Overviews), which is a 2026-specific planning layer.

## Key data points / quotes

- Solo founder cadence benchmark: "1-2 articles per week, 5 hours total."
- Founder + AI content engine: "2-3 articles per week, 5-8 hours."
- Small marketing team (2-3 people): "3-5 articles per week."
- "Teams planning 2-3 months ahead see 41% higher success rates."
- "Publishing 2 articles per week consistently beats publishing 8 one week and zero the next three."
- Content queue split: "70% evergreen cluster content, 30% timely/reactive pieces."
- Strategy map principle: "A content calendar is a schedule - what publishes when. A content strategy is the architecture behind it."
- Dual discovery planning: every piece planned for both Google and AI platforms.
- Queue ahead 4-6 weeks minimum for buffer.
- Batching method: "separate planning sessions from creation sessions... spend 1-2 hours per month on planning."

## Implications for stinger-forge

- The "5 hours/week" framing for solo founders is the correct capacity unit for `guides/07-cadence-planning.md` - more realistic than "X posts/month" targets divorced from time cost.
- The 70/30 evergreen/reactive split is a practical planning heuristic that the cadence planning guide should include.
- "Strategy map before calendar" is the correct sequencing instruction: the stinger should recommend building the cluster-pillar map before selecting publishing dates.
- The 4-6 week queue buffer recommendation prevents the "what do I publish this week?" panic that derails solo founder content programs.
- "41% higher success rates for 2-3 month ahead planning" is a concrete stat that motivates the forward-planning advice in the cadence guide.
